summary: Build-A-Bear has transitioned from a mall-based children's retailer into a diversified brand-equity business with a large, loyal ADULT fan base. Crucially, that adult demand is online- and community-led: grown fans engage through TikTok, Reddit, owned digital experiences and e-commerce rather than mall visits, and physical stores increasingly skew family and tourist traffic. This tracker monitors independent online demand signals — social, community, search, digital, and the secondary market — to assess whether adult-driven brand demand is durable and broadening.
convergence: Independent online channels — TikTok/owned social, the Reddit community, Roblox engagement, e-commerce demand and secondary-market pricing — point the same way. Convergence across unrelated platforms distinguishes a structural, adult-led demand shift from a single-channel fad.
bottom_line: Bottom line: multiple independent online signals — TikTok-led social, a compounding collector community, 24M+ Roblox visits, and four/five-figure resale clears — corroborate a real, adult-led demand base that is increasingly online and decoupled from mall foot traffic, while the P&L diversifies toward higher-margin commercial and franchising on record FY2025 revenue.
caveats: E-commerce demand is choppy quarter to quarter (Q3 FY25 −10.8%); the online channel is structural but not linear.
Search and the Reddit trajectory are partly modeled — Reddit has two verified anchors (27.4K→~37K); Search awaits a live Google Trends export.
Resale is a documented but small sample of notable sales, not a continuous price index.
Tariffs (~$11M in FY2025) and a maturing U.S. mall base are real margin/footprint headwinds.
